name: Garden tomato (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Garden tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) [Vegetables] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: DTU, MANUAL, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170457 (Tomatoes, red, ripe, raw, year round average; SR Legacy 11529)
de Sousa Ferreira et al. 2018, MethodsX / Heliyon 'Quantification of sugars and organic acids in tomato fruits' PMC6046607
Davies & Cocking 1972, J Sci Food Agric 23:969 doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740230808 (organic acids during ripening)
FooDB FOOD00171 (broad compound list; source of removed noise)
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are the two free hexoses glucose (~1.25 g/100g) + fructose (~1.37 g/100g) and the organic acid citric acid (~0.32 g/100g, the defining acid of tomato, formerly missing); malic acid is a minor secondary acid. Nitrogen is supplied largely by a large free amino-acid pool dominated by glutamate/aspartate plus GABA, not by bulk protein (only 0.88 g/100g). Values are per 100 g fresh red-ripe fruit; ~34 heavy/rare-metal and implausible FooDB entries removed as non-nutriti
curation_added: Citric acid (dominant organic acid, was absent)
GABA / 4-aminobutyrate (abundant free amino acid, was absent)
Explicit organic-acid and free-amino-acid pools that drive growth

curation_removed.name: Arsenate
curation_removed.reason: toxic non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Lead
curation_removed.reason: toxic trace contaminant, not a nutrient

curation_removed.name: Co2+
curation_removed.reason: ultratrace; cobalt not a measured tomato nutrient, FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Nickel
curation_removed.reason: ultratrace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Fluoride
curation_removed.reason: trace, not a microbial growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Iodine c
curation_removed.reason: negligible in tomato; not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Retinol
curation_removed.reason: animal-form vitamin A; tomato contains 0 retinol (provitamin-A supplied as carotenes) — implausible/mis-mapped

curation_removed.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
curation_removed.reason: duplicate/compartment-tagged iron entry; iron already captured as 'Iron'

curation_removed.name: Iron (Fe3+)
curation_removed.reason: duplicate iron speciation entry; folded into single 'Iron'

curation_removed.name: Maltose C12H22O11
curation_removed.reason: not a real free sugar of ripe tomato (hexose accumulator); FooDB artifact

curation_removed.name: D-Serine
curation_removed.reason: wrong stereochemistry; protein serine is L-serine (corrected)

curation_removed.name: Osmium
curation_removed.reason: non-nutritive rare metal, implausible, FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Palladium
curation_removed.reason: non-nutritive rare metal, implausible, FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: epsilon-Polylysine
curation_removed.reason: microbial-fermentation food additive/preservative, not a natural tomato constituent; implausible

curation_removed.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
curation_removed.reason: stereoisomer artifact of isoleucine, duplicate noise

curation_removed.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
curation_removed.reason: furanocoumarin of Apiaceae/citrus, biologically implausible in tomato; FooDB predicted
